project.primary_design_goal: Improve usability, diagnostics, safety, and extensibility of the LEGO Dimensions portal Python package without breaking the existing typed API.

important_terminology.portal: A physical LEGO Dimensions USB toy pad device.
important_terminology.pad: One of the three zones on a portal: centre, left, or right.
important_terminology.multi_unit: Future support for operating multiple physical USB portal devices at the same time. Do not confuse this with the existing three pad zones on one portal.

implementation_style: Prefer small, coherent changes with clear module boundaries.
Preserve existing public imports and backwards compatibility wherever practical.
Add new functionality behind new modules, CLI commands, or optional helper classes before changing existing behaviours.
Use dependency injection for USB access and gateway construction so features can be tested without real hardware.
When adding command line tools, support both human-readable output and machine-readable JSON where it is useful.
Keep hardware-touching operations safe by default.
Prefer dataclasses or TypedDict-like structures for diagnostic, event, preset, and capture records.
Use logging for developer diagnostics and stdout/stderr for user-facing CLI output.
multi_unit_future_constraints: Do not introduce new global singleton portal state.
Do not assume that exactly one USB portal will always exist.
Where new APIs identify a portal, use explicit names such as portal_id, device_id, serial_number, bus_address, or unit_id.
Where new APIs identify a zone on a portal, keep using Pad for centre/left/right pad zones.
Design event objects and file formats so a future portal_id can be added without breaking callers.

tests: Add or update pytest tests for new pure-Python logic.
Avoid requiring real USB hardware in CI.
typing: Use type hints for public functions and new dataclasses.
documentation: Update readme.md when adding user-facing commands.
Document dangerous operations and dry-run behaviour prominently.
packaging: Update pyproject.toml only when adding entry points, dependencies, project metadata, or optional extras.

tasks.title: Add a hardware diagnostic doctor command
tasks.summary: Create a new CLI command that diagnoses Python, PyUSB, libusb/backend availability, portal discovery, interface claiming, endpoint read/write behaviour, simple light control, and optional RFID packet reads.

tasks.title: Improve platform-specific installation and USB setup documentation
tasks.summary: Expand documentation for Linux, macOS, and Windows USB setup, including libusb installation, permissions, udev rules, kernel driver/interface claiming, and common troubleshooting paths.

tasks.title: Add safer tag backup, restore, and verify workflows
tasks.summary: Extend tag tooling so users can back up a tag, restore from a backup, and verify expected character/tag data before and after writes.

tasks.title: Add named character lookup and character-aware CLI operations
tasks.summary: Allow users to list and search character metadata and use character names where numeric IDs are currently required.

tasks.title: Add reusable light-show presets
tasks.summary: Create a preset system for common portal light patterns such as rainbow, pulse, police, identify, blank, tag-reactive, and custom preset files.

tasks.title: Add record and replay mode for tag and light sessions
tasks.summary: Allow users to record portal/tag events and optionally light commands to a session file, then replay sessions later for demos, debugging, or tests.

tasks.title: Add optional async APIs for event consumption and portal operations
tasks.summary: Create asyncio-friendly wrappers for tag events and selected gateway operations while preserving the existing synchronous API.

tasks.title: Add an optional local web UI
tasks.summary: Create a browser-based local control panel for device status, pad lights, live RFID events, character lookup, tag inspection, tag backup, preset light shows, and diagnostics.

tasks.title: Add structured logging and packet/session capture export
tasks.summary: Provide a consistent way to export packet reads/writes, tag events, diagnostic checks, and high-level commands to JSON or NDJSON for debugging and bug reports.

tasks.title: Clean up package metadata before broader publishing
tasks.summary: Review and correct package metadata such as homepage URL, license classifier, versioning policy, optional extras, project URLs, and README packaging behaviour.

suggested_new_abstractions.name: PortalIdentity
suggested_new_abstractions.purpose: Represent a physical USB portal in diagnostics, captures, logs, and future multi-unit routing.

suggested_new_abstractions.name: PortalManager
suggested_new_abstractions.purpose: Future manager for discovering and operating multiple physical portals.

suggested_new_abstractions.name: CaptureWriter
suggested_new_abstractions.purpose: Shared NDJSON writer for diagnostics, tag events, packet logs, and session recording.

suggested_new_abstractions.name: FakeGateway
suggested_new_abstractions.purpose: Reusable testing fake for CLI, presets, recorder, web UI, and async wrappers.

safety_defaults: Dry-run by default for tag writes/restores.
Blank pads after demos, diagnostics, presets, interrupted sessions, and exceptions unless explicitly disabled.
Bind web UI to 127.0.0.1 by default.
Fail rather than guess when hardware selection, character names, or tag identity are ambiguous.
future_multi_unit_roadmap: Phase 1: Add portal_id fields to new data models and file formats while still operating one portal.
Phase 2: Add discovery that can list multiple USB portal candidates.
Phase 3: Add explicit CLI targeting with --portal and --all-portals.
Phase 4: Add PortalManager capable of owning multiple Gateway instances.
Phase 5: Add concurrent async event streams per physical portal.
Phase 6: Add web UI portal selector and per-portal controls.
